name: Dr Keith Nowicki
focus: Infrared imaging • Optical system design • Emission spectroscopy
bio: Keith J. Nowicki is an Associate Professor of Physics at Northern Arizona University, where he teaches courses such as modern physics, physical optics, and undergraduate research while conducting work in applied optics and imaging systems. His research focuses on infrared imaging, optical system design, and emission spectroscopy, with applications in planetary science and space instrumentation. Dr. Nowicki has contributed to the development and testing of advanced optical systems for space missions, including instrumentation for telescopes and small satellite platforms aimed at studying planetary bodies. His work also explores laser-based spectroscopy, infrared data processing, and noise reduction techniques in imaging systems. Through a combination of experimental design and data analysis, he contributes to improving the accuracy and capability of optical instruments used in both laboratory and space environments, advancing research in planetary observation and remote sensing technologies.
